artifacts.summary:
## Overview
The meeting discussed the impact of COVID-19 on the tourism industry in Waikato, particularly in Waipa District, and the response plan to mitigate, restart, and reimagine the sector. The meeting also covered the financial situation of Hamilton Airport, the role of LES, and the LGFA's financial situation and plans.

## Key Discussion Points
### Tourism Industry
* The tourism industry in Waikato has been severely impacted by COVID-19, with a significant decline in international visitor arrivals and expenditure.
* The industry is expected to take at least 2-5 years to recover to pre-COVID levels.
* The Waikato Tourism sector has implemented a three-tiered response plan: mitigate, restart, and reimagine.
* The plan includes providing industry support, conducting one-on-one operator check-ins, and hosting webinars.
* The sector is also working on a major events restart campaign and a business events restart campaign.

### Hamilton Airport
* The airport has been severely impacted by COVID-19, with a significant decline in aeronautical income.
* The airport has completed significant cost reductions and applied for the government wage subsidy.
* The airport has a conservative model that assumes no Air New Zealand activity until October 2020.
* The airport has a major land sale that is unconditional and will provide 2.5 million dollars of margin of free cash flow into the group early next year.

### LES
* The LES has been working on transformative projects to help Waikato councils operate more efficiently.
* The organization is looking to be bolder and more disruptive to enable councils to transform how they operate.
* The LES has introduced a lot more careful tracking of what it is doing and whether it is being successful.
* The organization is working on key strategic projects, businesses as usual projects, and re-examining some of the work that it is doing.

### LGFA
* The LGFA has been working on providing a range of borrowing options to councils, including short-term and longer-term financing.
* The organization has completed a 1 billion dollar debt issue at 1.46% interest rate.
* The LGFA has a 1 billion dollar standby facility from the New Zealand government, which will be rolled over for a further period of time.
* The organization is working on introducing green financing to councils, which will allow them to borrow for projects that reduce emissions or have environmental outcomes.

## Decisions Made
* The meeting decided to accept the report on the health and safety program.
* The meeting moved into public excluded and went off-line.
* The meeting approved the recommendation for the LGFA's financial situation and plans.
* The meeting recommended that the LES continue its transformative projects to help Waikato councils operate more efficiently.

## Open Questions
* The meeting noted that the tourism industry will not recover to pre-COVID levels for at least 2-5 years.
* The meeting noted that the financial situation of Hamilton Airport is challenging, but the organization has a strong balance sheet and a conservative model that assumes no Air New Zealand activity until October 2020.
* The meeting noted that the LGFA's financial situation is also challenging, but the organization has a 1 billion dollar standby facility from the New Zealand government and has completed a 1 billion dollar debt issue at 1.46% interest rate.
* The meeting noted that the LES is working on transformative projects to help Waikato councils operate more efficiently, but the organization's success will depend on the commitment of the councils.
artifacts.followups:
## Email
Subject: Meeting Recap and Next Steps

Dear All,

I wanted to follow up on our meeting and provide a concise recap of the key decisions and next steps. The meeting focused on the impact of COVID-19 on the tourism industry, particularly in Waikato, and the response plan to mitigate, restart, and reimagine the sector.

Key decisions:

* The tourism industry will not recover to pre-COVID levels for at least 2-5 years.
* Hamilton Airport's financial situation is challenging, but the company has a strong balance sheet and a conservative model that assumes no Air New Zealand activity until October 2020.
* LGFA's financial situation is also challenging, but the organization has a 1 billion dollar standby facility from the New Zealand government and has completed a 1 billion dollar debt issue at 1.46% interest rate.

Next steps:

* The tourism sector will focus on a three-tiered response plan: mitigate, restart, and reimagine.
* The restart phase will involve research, business events restart campaign, and new developments.
* The reimagine phase will involve a reimagining of tourism, including a new plan domestically and internationally, and a focus on sustainable community-led tourism.

I encourage you to review the meeting transcript and the presentations from Jason, Mark, and Andrew for more information.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

## Slack
Key decisions and next steps:

* Tourism industry will not recover to pre-COVID levels for at least 2-5 years.
* Hamilton Airport and LGFA have a strong financial foundation, but face challenges due to COVID-19.
* Tourism sector will focus on a three-tiered response plan: mitigate, restart, and reimagine.
* Restart phase will involve research, business events restart campaign, and new developments.
* Reimagine phase will involve a reimagining of tourism, including a new plan domestically and internationally, and a focus on sustainable community-led tourism.

Next steps:

* Review meeting transcript and presentations for more information.
* Follow up with Jason, Mark, and Andrew for further discussion and updates.

## Email

Subject: Recap of Meeting on 26 July 2024

Dear All,

I wanted to follow up on the meeting held on 26 July 2024 to provide a concise recap of the key decisions and action items discussed.

The meeting covered various topics, including:

* Jason's presentation on the impact of COVID-19 on the tourism industry in Waikato, with a focus on the sector's response plan to mitigate, restart, and reimagine the industry.
* Mark Morgan's presentation on the financial situation of Hamilton Airport, including the impact of COVID-19 and the airport's response plan.
* Peter's presentation on the transformation role of the Local Authority Shared Services (LASS), including its vision and objectives.
* Andrew's presentation on the Local Government Funding Agency (LGFA), including its financial situation and plans.

Key decisions and action items include:

* Accepting the report on the health and safety program.
* Approving the recommendation for the LGFA's financial situation and plans.
* Moving into public excluded and going off-line to discuss further.

Please let me know if you have any questions or need further clarification on any of the points discussed.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

## Slack

Decision highlights: 
- Accepted report on the health and safety program
- Approved recommendation for LGFA's financial situation and plans
- Moved into public excluded and went off-line for further discussion

Action items: 
- Jason to continue working on the tourism industry response plan
- Mark to provide updates on Hamilton Airport's financial situation
- Peter to lead the transformation of LASS
- Andrew to provide updates on LGFA's financial situation and plans

## Overview
The meeting discusses the impact of COVID-19 on the tourism industry, particularly in Waikato, and the response plan to mitigate, restart, and reimagine the sector. The meeting also covers the financial situation of Hamilton Airport, the role of LES, and the LGFA's financial situation and plans. The participants include representatives from Waikato Tourism, Hamilton Airport, LES, LGFA, Waipa District Council, and other local government entities.

## Key Discussion Points
### Tourism Industry Impact
* The tourism industry in Waikato has been significantly impacted by COVID-19, with a loss of international visitor arrivals and a decline in visitor expenditure.
* The industry has been affected by the loss of China group travel, which started in January, and the closure of international borders.
* The sector has seen significant job losses and business closures, with many businesses going into temporary or seasonal closure.
* The industry is expecting a long recovery period, with a potential 2-5 year timeframe to regain pre-COVID levels.

### Response Plan
* The tourism industry has a three-tiered response plan, including mitigation, restart, and reimagine phases.
* The mitigation phase involves providing support to businesses and advocating for government support.
* The restart phase focuses on restarting the domestic market and promoting local tourism.
* The reimagine phase involves transforming the tourism industry to be more sustainable and community-led.

### Hamilton Airport
* The airport has been significantly impacted by COVID-19, with a 97-98% loss of aeronautical income.
* The airport has suspended the terminal upgrade work and is working with the government to access support.
* The airport has a conservative model that assumes no Air New Zealand activity until October 2020 and a 40% recovery of passenger numbers in 2021.

### LES
* The LES has been working on transformative projects to improve the organization's effectiveness and efficiency.
* The organization is committed to delivering security of public works, restraint on non-essential spending, and motivation and encouragement of council staff.
* The LES is working with councils to advance strategic projects and improve the well-being of the community.

### LGFA
* The LGFA has been providing financial support to councils during the COVID-19 pandemic.
* The organization has a 1 billion dollar standby facility from the New Zealand government and has completed a 1 billion dollar debt issue at 1.46% interest rate.
* The LGFA is working on introducing green financing and environmental bonds to support councils in reducing emissions and achieving environmental outcomes.

## Decisions Made
* The meeting decides to accept the report on the health and safety program.
* The meeting approves the recommendation for the LGFA's financial situation and plans.
* The meeting moves into public excluded and goes off-line.

## Open Questions
* The meeting notes that the tourism industry will not recover to pre-COVID levels for at least 2-5 years.
* The meeting notes that the financial situation of Hamilton Airport is challenging, but the company has a strong balance sheet and a conservative model that assumes no Air New Zealand activity until October 2020.
* The meeting notes that the LGFA's financial situation is also challenging, but the organization has a 1 billion dollar standby facility from the New Zealand government and has completed a 1 billion dollar debt issue at 1.46% interest rate.
* The meeting notes that the tourism industry is expecting a long recovery period and that the sector will need to transform to be more sustainable and community-led.

## Email
Subject: Follow-up on Waikato Tourism and Economic Recovery Meeting

Dear All,

I wanted to follow up on the meeting held on [date] regarding the impact of COVID-19 on the tourism industry in Waikato and the response plan to mitigate, restart, and reimagine the sector. As discussed, the meeting covered the financial situation of Hamilton Airport, the role of LES, and the LGFA's financial situation and plans.

Key decisions and action items from the meeting include:

* Acceptance of the report on the health and safety program
* Approval of the recommendation for the LGFA's financial situation and plans
* Discussion on the impact of COVID-19 on the tourism industry, particularly in Waikato
* Review of the financial situation of Hamilton Airport and the role of LES
* Introduction of the LGFA's financial situation and plans

I would like to thank Jason, Mark, and Scott for their presentations and for sharing their expertise on the current situation and the response plan. I also appreciate the input from all participants and the discussion on the key issues affecting the tourism industry in Waikato.

If you have any further questions or would like to discuss any of the topics covered in the meeting, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

Key decisions and action items from the meeting:

## Overview
The meeting discussed the impact of COVID-19 on the tourism industry, particularly in Waikato, and the response plan to mitigate, restart, and reimagine the sector. The tourism industry is expected to take at least 2-5 years to recover to pre-COVID levels. The meeting also covered the financial situation of Hamilton Airport, the role of LES, and the LGFA's financial situation and plans.

## Key Discussion Points
### Tourism Industry
* The tourism industry in Waikato was significantly impacted by COVID-19, with a loss of international visitor arrivals and a decline in visitor expenditure.
* The industry is expected to take at least 2-5 years to recover to pre-COVID levels.
* The Waikato Tourism sector has a three-tiered response plan: mitigate, restart, and reimagine.
* The mitigate phase involves providing support to businesses, including advocacy at a national level and one-on-one operator check-ins.
* The restart phase involves focusing on the future and how to restart the region, including research and a major business events restart campaign.
* The reimagine phase involves reimagining the tourism industry, including a new plan domestically and internationally, and a focus on sustainable community-led tourism.

### Hamilton Airport
* The airport's financial situation is challenging due to the suspension of flights and the impact on the aeronautical business.
* The airport has completed significant cost reductions and applied for the government wage subsidy.
* The airport has a conservative model that assumes no Air New Zealand activity until October 2020 and a 40% recovery of passenger numbers in 2021.
* The airport has a major land sale that is unconditional and will provide 2.5 million dollars of margin of free cash flow into the group early next year.

### LES
* The LES has a new chair, Peter, who introduced himself and discussed the organization's transformation role.
* The LES aims to create effectiveness and efficiency gains for shareholders, increase the influence of the region, and provide a better customer experience.
* The LES is working on several strategic projects, including energy and carbon management, and is re-examining some of its work to see whether it should stop doing it.
* The LES is committed to delivering its work program as set out in the statement of intent, including security of public works, restraint on non-essential spending, and motivation and encouragement of council staff.

### LGFA
* The LGFA has 67 member councils and provides a range of borrowing options, including short-term and longer-term financing.
* The LGFA has a 1 billion dollar standby facility from the New Zealand government and has completed a 1 billion dollar debt issue at 1.46% interest rate.
* The LGFA is working on green financing and environmental bonds, which will allow councils to borrow for projects that reduce emissions or have environmental outcomes.
* The LGFA is committed to providing a range of borrowing options to councils, including green financing, and is working with councils to identify green projects.

## Decisions Made
* The meeting accepted the report on the health and safety program.
* The meeting moved into public excluded and went off-line.

## Open Questions
* The meeting did not discuss any specific open questions or unresolved items.
